action: Implement and test the fail-closed effective queue reconciliation gate defined by M-009.

observed: M-009 specified eight fail-closed invariants but no executable implementation existed.
The new gate binds output to immutable source and input identities, rejects count mismatch and malformed records, and emits ambiguous precedence contradictions.
inferred: Fail-closed reconciliation reduces silent history omission and false task authority when canonical and append-only queue records diverge.
proposed: Add repository-native queue-update discovery and require this gate before every autonomous task selection.
superseded: Direct queue selection from ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone.
Fail-open merging of a caller-provided update subset.
unresolved: Complete repository directory enumeration and blob-to-commit membership remain outside the current connector surface.
CI and team-runtime wiring remain incomplete.

verification: Six deterministic Node tests passed with zero failures before commit, covering acceptance, omission, mutable source identity, unsupported schema, ambiguous precedence, and undiscovered parsed paths.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata and abstract operational structure only; raw private chat, health material, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
handoff: Continuity Mining should next implement repository-native corpus discovery at one immutable commit, bind each blob to that commit, and wire the effective queue gate into CI and autonomous team startup.
